rule: hit = (kw>=2) OR (sem>=0.7) OR (func>=0.7); independent per-result scoring; total_hits aggregates across results; threshold_for_fail=1
candidate_summary: VENIK-OSCILLATION: imposed alternating sign-flipped activations every M=10 steps, weighted (1-lambda, lambda) average of parameter updates across standard and sign-flipped passes, fixed M-step cadence. Claims novelty by recombining (i) imposed sign-flip alternation + (ii) weighted update average + (iii) fixed cadence.

results_analysis.title: Duality Between SAM and Adversarial Training (2402.15152)
results_analysis.reasoning: SAM/adversarial-training duality establishes the same imposed alternation primal-dual cycle (perturb-then-minimize) that VENIK uses. Sign-flip is a specific instance of the perturbation step; weighted average across two passes mirrors SAM's gradient combination. All three signals (kw>=2, sem>=0.7, func>=0.7) fire.

results_analysis.title: Stabilizing SAM (JMLR 2025)
results_analysis.matched_effect_cluster: SAM oscillation cycle along principal Hessian direction
results_analysis.reasoning: Paper explicitly characterizes SAM as converging to a CYCLE OSCILLATING between min/max along principal Hessian direction with alternating perturb-then-minimize. This is exactly VENIK's imposed alternating-sign cycle mechanism. All three rules trigger.

results_analysis.title: Friendly Sharpness-Aware Minimization
results_analysis.reasoning: Two-batch alternation inside SAM perturb-minimize loop = imposed fixed-cadence alternation between two forward modes with combined update, matching VENIK's (i)+(ii)+(iii) recombination. All three rules trigger.

results_analysis.title: Sparse Layer SAM for Efficient Fine-Tuning (2602.09395)
results_analysis.reasoning: Sparse-layer SAM applies SAM's alternating perturb-minimize specifically to LLM fine-tune (same domain as VENIK). The 'every other layer' aspect of VENIK's sign-flip overlaps with sparse-layer selection. All three rules trigger.

results_analysis.title: SAM Alone Improves Adversarial Robustness
results_analysis.matched_effect_cluster: SAM Alone adversarial robustness via alternation
results_analysis.reasoning: Documents that SAM's perturbation alternation alone yields robustness gains - same imposed-alternation training mechanism. All three rules trigger.

results_analysis.title: GPAS: Gradient-Preserving Activation Scaling (2506.22049)
results_analysis.reasoning: Direct modulation of intermediate activations (scaling) while preserving gradients = adjacent mechanism to VENIK's sign-flip of pre-norm hidden states. Functional score below threshold, but kw>=2 and sem>=0.7 both trigger -> overall hit.

results_analysis.title: Evolution Strategies at Hyperscale
results_analysis.reasoning: ES with single perturbation direction + two function queries averaged for memory-efficient LLM fine-tune mirrors VENIK's two-mode forward pass + weighted-average update. Func slightly below threshold, but kw>=2 and sem>=0.7 trigger.

results_analysis.title: PIP: Perturbation-based Iterative Pruning (EMNLP 2025)
results_analysis.reasoning: Off-domain (pruning, not training alternation). No rule fires.

verdict_sentence: VENIK-OSCILLATION fails novelty: 7 of 8 retrieved results are hits (5 functional >=0.7, 7 semantic >=0.7, 7 kw>=2), with multi-cluster overlap on SAM perturb-minimize alternation cycles (2402.15152, JMLR Stabilizing SAM, Friendly SAM, Sparse-Layer SAM, SAM-Alone), activation modulation (GPAS), and ES two-query averaged fine-tune; the candidate's three claimed components (imposed alternation + weighted average + fixed cadence) are jointly covered by prior art, far exceeding the threshold_for_fail=1.
